title: Dandi Beach
tagline: Dandi Beach — beach town of Gujarat

overview.short: Dandi Beach is one of the prominent beaches located in Dandi village, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: Dandi Beach is one of the prominent beaches located in Dandi village, Gujarat, India. Dandi beach is one of the cleanest beaches in the Arabian Sea. Dandi Beach is historically prominent as Mahatma Gandhi led the salt sathyagraha from Sabarmati Ashram (Ahmedabad) to Dandi. This is the beach where Mahatma Gandhi broke the salt tax law of the British after the Salt March.

topPlaces.name: Dandi, Navsari
topPlaces.description: Dandi is a village in the Jalalpore taluka, Navsari District, Gujarat, India. It is located on the coast of the Arabian Sea near the city of Navsari.

topPlaces.name: National Salt Satyagraha Memorial
topPlaces.description: The National Salt Satyagraha Memorial or Dandi Memorial is a memorial in Dandi, Gujarat, India, that honors the activists and participants of the Salt Satyagraha, an act of nonviolent civil disobedience in colonial India which was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930. The memorial is spread over a 15 acres (61,000 m2) and is located in the coastal town of Dandi, where the Salt March ended on 5 April 1930 and the British salt monopoly was broken by producing salt by boiling sea water.

topPlaces.name: Purna River (Gujarat)
topPlaces.description: Purna is one of the major rivers in the Indian state of Gujarat, though its source lies at the hill of Pipalner in the Sahyadri range in Maharashtra. It then passes through three districts in Gujarat, including Dang, Surat and Navsari.

items.note: Dandi is a village in the Jalalpore taluka, Navsari District, Gujarat, India. It is located on the coast of the Arabian Sea near the city of Navsari.

The National Salt Satyagraha Memorial or Dandi Memorial is a memorial in Dandi, Gujarat, India, that honors the activists and participants of the Salt Satyagraha, an act of nonviolent civil disobedience in colonial India which was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930. The memorial is spread over a 15 acres (61,000 m2) and is located in the coastal town of Dandi, where the Salt March ended on 5 April 1930 and the British salt monopoly was broken by producing salt by boiling sea water.

Purna is one of the major rivers in the Indian state of Gujarat, though its source lies at the hill of Pipalner in the Sahyadri range in Maharashtra. It then passes through three districts in Gujarat, including Dang, Surat and Navsari.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dandi Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dandi Beach is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dandi Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Surat Airport (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Surat Railway Station (37 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dandi Beach?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dandi Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dandi Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Dandi Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dandi Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dandi, Navsari, National Salt Satyagraha Memorial, Purna River (Gujarat).
